title: NEO 4.0 P4-AUTONOMY C4 per-slice external-call accounting entry (one JSONL line)
_home: <RunRoot>/external_slice_call_ledger.jsonl - append-only, one shared file keyed by slice_id (S1 attempt-ledger pattern); monotonic slice_call_seq per slice; WRITE-AHEAD: the non-refused entry lands BEFORE the process launches; fail-closed read discipline identical to the S1 ledgers
_status: DRAFT 4.0-P4-C4 (schema gate pending). Dispatch 3.A.3 SHAPE RULE (R5-F1/R6-F1/R7-F1, one rule) encoded fail-closed via allOf+if/then over the supported keyword set ('not' is outside the installed validator; ABSENT-property is enforced by empty-enum, which fails whenever the property is PRESENT): (1) NON-refused consumed call (refused=false): reason=NONE + post_increment_count + run_ledger_ref_kind=CONSUMED REQUIRED; (2) SUB-CAP refusal (reason=CAP_EXTERNAL_SLICE): NO run-ledger reference AT ALL (post_increment_count and run_ledger_ref_kind must be ABSENT - none exists by design); (3) RUN-CAP refusal (reason=CAP_EXTERNAL_CALLS): the REFUSING post_increment_count REQUIRED + run_ledger_ref_kind=REFUSED (write-ahead evidence of the landed refused run-ledger entry, never presentable as a successful consumption). round_id + bundle_diff_hash are required in ALL shapes (no optional escape hatches; a refusal records WHICH round/bundle attempt was refused - R3-F1 binding for consumed calls, honest END evidence for refusals). No refused entry ever satisfies a GO correlation (derivation requires refused=false AND run_ledger_ref_kind=CONSUMED). LEDGER_FAILURE is deliberately NOT a reason: a ledger failure appends NOTHING (fixture 17).
